description: Hand-logged sheet of businesses that enquired about VAT registration before the law passed. Maintained by front desk. Started Sep 2024.
schemaNote: Free-form. Not linked to GRT numbers. Names as given verbally — may not match legal name. Officer initials only.
rows: Called asking about VAT timeline. Wants to be ready. Will register immediately when law passes.
Came in person with assistant. Asked about input credits on imports — very keen. US military contractor.
Sarah — the dive place near the lagoon. Asked if equipment imports will be refundable. We said probably yes. Kathy's contact.
Called from Ebeye. Said 'we import a lot of food ingredients so want to get the money back'. Wants voluntary registration. No email.
Young guy, came in. Building contractor. Said he imports all his materials. Interested in refunds. New to the area.
Alvin — new import business. Said he's bringing in a full container at start of Q1. Wants to claim input tax back immediately.
Ben from RMI Construction. Large company. Currently working on school upgrade project. Expects big material imports Q1. Sophisticated — has accountant.
Small solar installer. Imports all panels and batteries. Interested in voluntary registration. Seems genuine.
Small events company. Wants to register to look legitimate to hotel clients. Low turnover.
Sam — electrical supplies importer. Input credits on specialised components. Voluntary registration makes sense for them.
Lady called about fisheries — not sure which company. Phone only. No follow-up.
This is the Pacific Tourism Services place near the wharf. Kathy arranged the meeting. Very keen to be ready. Manager is Carlos? Or Kelly? Need to confirm.
Same guy as Oct 2. Called back. Still interested. Mentioned he might have a big refund in first quarter.
Nina — agricultural supplies. Imports fertiliser and tools. No phone noted. Wants refund on imports. No email either.
Dr Grace — imports medical equipment. Very clear about what she wants. Well-organised. Will register voluntarily.
Rose — Ebeye. Imports food. Paper everything — no computer. Will file manually.
Rachel — runs a catering/food service operation. Large food imports. Asked about refunds. Seemed to have high expectations.
Jack called again re VAT. Still no computer. Will file everything on paper. Fine.
Mark — freight forwarder. Wants to register so clients can get VAT invoices. Not really about refunds — more about B2B invoicing.
Kim — cleaning products. Import business. Major clients are hotels — needs VAT invoices to keep their business.
